findings.summary: verify_peer_confirmation ignores its my_role argument and accepts either the client_finished or server_finished HMAC label. This breaks the role separation described in the protocol comment: a client's own HMAC(shared_secret, "client_finished") can be reflected back and accepted as the peer's server confirmation, so the client can believe the server confirmed the key when no server_finished tag was produced.
findings.evidence: Running the repro prints that the vulnerable verifier accepts a reflected client tag while a role-strict verifier rejects it, then exits successfully with 'BUG: client accepts its own confirmation tag as the server confirmation'.
